steps.title: Spot the defining claim
steps.content: The key signal here is “did not play drums on the label’s classic song ‘Rapper’s _______,’ he joined the label after the song’s release.” This sentence structure presents a clear sequence of events where one event clearly follows another, indicating a precise timing or sequence.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
C captures the specific relationship between two independent clauses with a semicolon because it correctly separates “Delight,” from “He joined the label after the song’s release,” which are both full sentences.

The other three incorrectly use different punctuation that does not maintain the separation and logical sequence needed — they either misuse commas or introduce unnecessary elements like period separators.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
